pattern: Count ordered index pairs where the shorter word is simultaneously a prefix and a suffix of the longer word; brute-force O(n²) works given the small constraints.
hints: For each pair (i, j) with i < j, check whether words[i] starts words[j] and also ends words[j].
A string s1 is a prefix of s2 if s2.startswith(s1), and a suffix if s2.endswith(s1); only count when len(s1) <= len(s2).
With at most 50 words, a nested loop over all (i, j) pairs is well within limits.

edge_inputs.description: single word — no valid pair possible
word is prefix but not suffix
word is suffix but not prefix
all words are the same character
longer word first — ordering matters, i < j required
no pair qualifies — all words same length and different

stress.description: random short lowercase words up to length 10
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = size
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 10)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(length)))
    return [words]
